text: `propose-change` doesn't survey existing in-flight `propose-change` branches or open spec-changing PRs before authoring a new proposal, which lets concurrent propose-change sessions independently commit to conflicting architectural solutions for the same problem area. Observed 2026-05-23: two competing v071 designs for cross-repo work-item dep awareness landed on different branches — PR #160 (`spec/revise-v071-next-and-cross-repo-deps`, opened 2026-05-22) chose the resolver-skill design (new `depends_on_external` + `external_dep_cache` fields, new `/livespec:resolve-cross-repo-deps` skill, `next` stays local-only); the later-authored `cross-project-interdependencies` propose-change (merged to master as commit `38e9562`) chose the syntactic-extension design (extend existing `depends_on` with `<repo-slug>:<work-item-id>` colon form, widen `next` to walk all manifest stores). The two designs are architecturally opposed on the line-252 pin ("next stays local-only"). Neither propose-change session surveyed the other; neither revise session had visibility into the other (PR #160's revise ran on its feature branch before `cross-project-interdependencies` was filed; my session's revise on master had no signal that PR #160 was in flight). Steering at revise can catch the symptom retroactively, but the deeper structural fix is at propose-change: enumerate open propose-change branches + open spec-changing PRs before authoring, so the new proposal either aligns, modifies, or explicitly supersedes the in-flight design. Related: the `/livespec:revise` SKILL.md already enumerates sibling sub-spec proposed_changes/ queues for cross-tree narration — extending this discipline to "across-branch in-flight propose-change drift" is a natural widening.

Investigate adding a Claude Code hook (UserPromptSubmit or PreToolUse, configured via `settings.json`) that intercepts any attempt by the agent to save observations into Claude Code's local auto-memory (`~/.claude/projects/.../memory/` Write tool calls) and routes them through `/livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo` instead. Motivation: local auto-memory is per-machine + per-user transient state, lost when the project is cloned elsewhere or when memory is pruned; `memos.jsonl` is checked in, durable, and flows through the canonical `process-memos` triage (spec-bound → propose-change, impl-bound → work-item, persistent-knowledge → store, discard). Observed 2026-05-23: in this session I twice reached for local auto-memory (the agent-level habit) when the user-correct path was `capture-memo` — the user had to explicitly redirect both times ("don't save a memory — use our capture memo skill"). A mechanical hook would remove the redirection burden and enforce the impl-plugin's memo discipline as the default deposit path. Open questions for the investigation: (a) which hook event fires earliest in the Write-tool path so the hook can substitute the call rather than just blocking it, (b) whether the hook should fire on every Write to `*/memory/*.md` or only when the agent's free-text observation pattern is detected, (c) whether to make the hook livespec-plugin-scoped (only fires when the project carries `.livespec.jsonc` declaring an impl-plugin with `memos_path`) or globally on, (d) how to surface the substitution to the user so the agent's intent stays visible.

Update `livespec-impl-plaintext:capture-memo`'s SKILL.md so the agent reaches for it instead of Claude Code's local auto-memory without needing a hook. Five concrete gaps surfaced 2026-05-23 (this session, after the user had to redirect twice from local-auto-memory to capture-memo):

(1) **No trigger-phrase vocabulary overlap with the agent's auto-memory reflex.** Global agent instructions train a specific vocabulary — "save a memory", "remember this", "save feedback", "note for future", "for future sessions" — and the current SKILL.md never names any of those. Pattern-matching never fires. Add explicit trigger phrases to the description or a `## When to use` section.

(2) **No "supersedes Claude Code local auto-memory" assertion.** The SKILL.md is silent on the existence of the global auto-memory system. In livespec-governed projects (any project carrying `.livespec.jsonc`) capture-memo MUST claim authority over the deposit path. Compare to `update-config`'s description in the available-skills list, which routes correctly because it says "memory/preferences cannot fulfill them".

(3) **The "transient by construction" framing actively steers AWAY for observations that feel important.** "Transient" reads as opposite-of-durable, when the actual durability hierarchy is inverted: memos are checked into the repo and flow through `process-memos` to durable artifacts (propose-change, work-item, persistent-knowledge file). Local auto-memory is the truly transient option (per-machine, lost on clone). Reframe so the durability story is upfront, not buried under "transient".

(4) **No proactive-use routing hint in description / frontmatter.** Skill reads as user-typed slash command only. Add proactive-use language so the agent invokes it without waiting for user redirection ("invoke proactively when you would otherwise save to local auto-memory").

(5) **No examples that pattern-match the agent's mid-task observation moment.** Current `## Flow` starts with "Ask for the memo text" — the user-initiated path. Add an example/section for the agent-initiated reflex (agent mid-task, notices something unrelated, would have saved a memory — capture-memo instead).

**Cheap, high-impact fix:** one paragraph at the top of the description (or new `## When to use` body section) — names the reflex vocabulary, asserts supersession in livespec-governed projects, partitions the user-profile-fact vs project-observation boundary so persistent user facts (role, preferences) can still flow through local auto-memory while project observations go through capture-memo. Belt-and-suspenders combination with the hook (memo `mm-3orvuu`): SKILL.md text alone won't override deeply-trained habits across all sessions, but the hook is overkill for in-livespec-project usage if the SKILL.md is well-written. Both are worth doing; SKILL.md fix is cheaper and is a prerequisite for the hook to be merely belt-and-suspenders rather than load-bearing.

doctor's out-of-band-edits auto-backfill writes into v(N+1) where N is read from git HEAD, but during a just-completed revise's post-step the working tree already has v(N+1) cut from the in-flight revise — so the auto-backfill files land INSIDE that just-cut directory instead of v(N+2), clashing with the legitimate revision files.

Symptoms observed during v072 cut (2026-05-23): cross-repo-dependency-awareness-revision.md correctly placed in v072/proposed_changes/, but doctor's post-step auto-backfill then wrote out-of-band-edit-2026-05-23t16-41-35z-{.md,-revision.md} into the SAME v072 directory because it computed N=71 from git HEAD (where v072 isn't committed yet). The auto-backfill also produced an inconsistent revision file (claimed README.md changed but byte-content was identical to v071's README.md), triggering doctor-accept-decision-snapshot-consistency to fail on the snapshot it just wrote.

The fix is for the auto-backfill to use working-tree state (or post-revise state) to determine the next version slot, not git HEAD. Alternative: skip the auto-backfill when running as the post-step of a just-completed revise (the revise already absorbed the drift into v(N+1)).

See .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/doctor/static/_out_of_band_edits_writes.py and the parent out_of_band_edits.py for the check implementation.

Seed wrapper (bin/seed.py) REWRITES .livespec.jsonc to a minimal {"template": "..."} form on every invocation, clobbering any hand-authored content (compat blocks, implementation key, cross_repo_targets, user comments). Observed during livespec-runtime sibling-library bootstrap (/livespec:seed against pre-authored .livespec.jsonc with compat metadata). Worked around in livespec-runtime PR #3 by `git checkout .livespec.jsonc` after the wrapper exits. Real fix: seed wrapper should MERGE template-required keys (template, spec_root) into the existing config rather than overwriting the whole file. Sibling libraries needing pre-authored config (compat, cross_repo_targets, implementation) are increasingly common — this bug blocks the canonical sibling-bootstrap flow.

BLOCKER: li-f5wmjr piece 2 (three impl-side cleanup invariants — no-stale-merged-branch, no-stale-merged-pr-branch, no-stale-worktree) cannot be implemented as written. The contract at SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Impl-side cleanup invariants (cross-boundary)" requires each invariant to fire `warn` (not `fail`) — but Finding.status's allowed values are pass/fail/skipped per .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec/schemas/finding.schema.json. There is no `warn` slot in the schema. The same gap blocks `no-stale-gap-tied` (also spec'd as `warn` in contracts.md, also unimplemented).

Resolution paths (pick one via propose-change):
  (A) Extend finding.schema.json's status enum to add `warn`; update       Finding dataclass + downstream doctor static-phase output       contract (exit-code mapping: `warn` likely → exit 0 with stderr       narration; `fail` stays exit 3).
  (B) Re-classify cleanup invariants away from doctor: surface them via       `<impl-plugin>:list-work-items` auto-filed work-items, or as a       separate `<impl-plugin>:doctor-cleanup` skill that's not a       structural invariant.
  (C) Drop the cleanup-invariants section from contracts.md; rely on       manual `git branch -d` / `git worktree remove` hygiene.

Recommend (A) since the spec already has multiple warn-class invariants in contracts.md (no-stale-gap-tied, the three cleanup ones); the schema extension is one-line and unblocks all of them at once.

Until resolved: li-f5wmjr piece 2 is blocked. Piece 1 (next ranker + ready filter wiring) landed via impl-plaintext PR #15. The data migration (piece 3) landed via PR #14 + livespec PR #174.
